channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why OpenAI bought 'SportsCenter for Silicon Valley'
item.description: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: ICE acknowledges it is using powerful spyware
item.description: In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress that the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.
item.content:encoded:
In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress that the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.

(Image credit: Octavio Jones)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Building AI bots becomes the latest viral craze in China
item.description: A fascination with AI bots, made with a program called OpenClaw, is sweeping China.
item.content:encoded: A fascination with AI bots, made with a program called OpenClaw, is sweeping China.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Amid a high-profile scandal, Germany considers deepfake porn punishments
item.description: Congress passed the Take It Down Act in 2024, protecting victims of deepfake revenge pornography. Now, Germany is considering punishing the creators of deepfake porn, not just the distributors, for up to 2 years. NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Harvard Law Professor Rebecca Tushnet.
item.content:encoded: Congress passed the Take It Down Act in 2024, protecting victims of deepfake revenge pornography. Now, Germany is considering punishing the creators of deepfake porn, not just the distributors, for up to 2 years. NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Harvard Law Professor Rebecca Tushnet.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Verdicts against Meta and Google may bring a new era of big tech accountability
item.description: Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.
item.content:encoded:
Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Big tech's next move is to put data centers in space. Can it work?
item.description: In orbit, power is free. But everything else is expensive.
item.content:encoded: In orbit, power is free. But everything else is expensive.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: What's next for Meta in the wake of trial losses and layoffs?
item.description: The company behind Facebook and Instagram has lost two major court cases and appears to be scaling back on the virtual reality Metaverse.
item.content:encoded:
The company behind Facebook and Instagram has lost two major court cases and appears to be scaling back on the virtual reality Metaverse.

(Image credit: Tony Avelar)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Trump administration sues three states over attempts to regulate prediction markets
item.description: The suits are the most ambitious effort to date that the Trump administration has gone to try to override state laws and set the rules for the fast-growing and increasingly divisive betting industry.
item.content:encoded:
The suits are the most ambitious effort to date that the Trump administration has gone to try to override state laws and set the rules for the fast-growing and increasingly divisive betting industry.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: From scrappy startup to tech giant, Apple celebrates its 50th year
item.description: Countercultural mythmaking and global corporate dominance have helped the tech corporation sail through criticism.
item.content:encoded:
Countercultural mythmaking and global corporate dominance have helped the tech corporation sail through criticism.

(Image credit: Paul Sakuma)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: China's AI chatbots are advanced and versatile — and begging for more users
item.description: Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.
item.content:encoded:
Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.

(Image credit: ADEK BERRY/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch
